question: Tell me about a time you led a cross-functional initiative under tight constraints.
candidateAnswer: At Acme, our checkout team had a 12% drop-off at the payment step that was costing us about $400k/quarter. I led a six-week effort with engineering, design, and risk to fix it. I started by interviewing the support team to surface the top three failure modes, then built a prioritized backlog and got buy-in from each function lead in a single working session. I owned the daily stand-up, unblocked the team on a vendor API issue by escalating to our Stripe rep, and shipped a redesigned payment step plus a retry flow. We measured a 38% reduction in drop-off in the first two weeks, recovering an estimated $150k in the first month. I learned that aligning on the metric upfront made every later trade-off easy.
idealFollowUpExamples: What pushback did you face from any of the function leads?
